steps.title: Identify what the blank needs
steps.content: The sentence reads: "exploring the geology of the red planet, including its many ______ To aid in communication..." The phrase "its many ______" requires plain plural nouns naming geological features. A possessive form would need something after it that the noun owns — nothing follows here.

steps.title: Test each choice for possessive versus plural
steps.content:
• Choice A — craters and rock's.: "rock's" is a possessive but nothing follows for it to possess; incorrect apostrophe use. ✗
  • Choice B — craters and rocks.: both are simple plurals naming the geological features the lander explored — exactly what "its many ______" requires. ✓
  • Choice C — crater's and rocks.: "crater's" is a possessive with nothing following it to possess. ✗
  • Choice D — crater's and rock's.: both words are possessives; neither can possess anything here. ✗
B is correct.
